QF.id: XIII Introduction to the Underlying Markets_43

question.text: Which of the following is NOT a feature of exchange-traded currency derivatives in India?

answer.text: Customizable contract sizes based on individual hedging needs
Standardized contract specifications including lot size and expiry dates
Central counterparty guarantee eliminating counterparty credit risk
Daily mark-to-market settlement through the clearing corporation

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Exchange-traded derivatives have standardized (not customizable) contract sizes. USDINR futures have a fixed lot of USD 1,000. Customization is a feature of OTC forwards, not exchange-traded products. **The Trap:** Standardization is the trade-off for exchange trading — you get CCP guarantee and liquidity but lose flexibility. OTC forwards offer customization but carry counterparty risk. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series I Ch2-3 compare exchange-traded vs OTC currency derivatives. The same distinction applies in equity (Series VIII) and IR (Series IV) markets. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The standardization constraint means hedgers often have basis risk — the hedge may not perfectly match the underlying exposure in amount or timing.
